title: Boat Rental in Antwerp Schelde: Costs and What to Know
html:
To rent a boat in Antwerp on the Schelde, set off from Willemdok marina in the old docklands and expect a day price band of roughly EUR 250 to EUR 1,400 depending on the craft. BoatHire24 lets you compare local skippers, self-drive motorboats and crewed yachts in one place.

Where to set off in Antwerp Schelde

The Schelde is a broad tidal river, so most rentals start from a sheltered dock rather than the open stream. Antwerp keeps its leisure fleet in the regenerated northern docklands, a short walk from the historic centre and the cathedral.

  • Willemdok: the main marina, tucked behind the MAS museum and a natural meeting point for skippered trips.
  • Bonapartedok: the adjacent inner dock, handy for early morning departures.
  • Kattendijkdok: a larger basin used by sailing yachts and motor cruisers.
  • Lillo: a small polder village downstream, a popular turnaround point on the lower Schelde.
  • Hemiksem: a quieter quay upstream towards Rupelmonde, good for calmer water.

Which boat to rent in Antwerp Schelde

Choose your craft around the tide and the wind, because the Schelde runs fast and can be choppy when an estuary breeze meets the current. Self-drive day boats suit calm spells, while a skippered yacht is the relaxed option for a group that simply wants the view.

  1. Open motorboat: best for a half-day pootle past the skyline, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 180.
  2. Speedboat: ideal for covering the river quickly to Lillo and back, seats 5 to 7 guests, from EUR 350; this speedboat is a typical Axopar-style example.
  3. Sailing yacht: a steady choice for a full day on the tide, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 450.
  4. Motor cruiser: roomy and weatherproof for mixed conditions, seats up to 10 guests, from EUR 600.
  5. Crewed yacht: the hands-off option with a local skipper, seats up to 12 guests, from EUR 900.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Antwerp Schelde?

Expect roughly EUR 35 per seat for a shared trip and up to EUR 1,400 for a private crewed yacht across a full day. Prices in 2026 vary with crew, fuel and season, so it pays to compare. You can compare quotes and book a boat online once you have settled on a date and group size.

  • Shared river tour: from about EUR 35 per person for a couple of hours.
  • Small motorboat, half-day: from about EUR 180 in EUR for up to six guests.
  • Catamaran, full-day: from about EUR 700 in EUR, weather permitting.
  • Crewed yacht, full-day: from about EUR 900 and rising to EUR 1,400 in EUR.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Antwerp?

The high season runs from May to September, when daylight is long and the river is at its most inviting. Outside those months the water turns cold and grey, and many self-drive operators reduce their fleet. The dominant local wind is the south-westerly that funnels up the estuary from the North Sea, which can build a short, lumpy chop on an outgoing tide.

Plan your run with the flood tide if you are heading downstream towards Lillo, around 12 nautical miles from the city docks, so the current carries you out and home rather than fighting you both ways. Belgium follows the country principle that motorised pleasure craft above a certain length or power need a recognised certificate, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat before you sail.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 35 per seat to EUR 1,400 per day.
  • Peak months: May to September.
  • Trip length: 2 hours to a full day.
  • Capacity: typically 5 to 12 guests.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Departure: Willemdok and the northern docklands.

Questions about boat rental in Antwerp Schelde

Do I need a licence to drive a rental boat in Antwerp?

It depends on the boat, because Belgium ties the requirement to length and engine power. Many small self-drive day boats can be hired without a formal certificate, while larger or faster craft need one, so the operator confirms before you book.

Is the Schelde safe for a first-time renter?

The Schelde is a working tidal river with commercial traffic and strong currents, so first-timers are best served by a skippered trip or a calm-weather slot. Local operators brief you fully and will decline to launch in unsafe conditions.

How far can I cruise in a single day?

A comfortable day run reaches Lillo downstream or Rupelmonde upstream and back, roughly 12 nautical miles each way. Tides and engine size set the realistic limit, and a skipper plans the route around the flood and ebb.

Antwerp pairs a grand maritime city with a genuinely open river, and a half-day on the Schelde is an easy, memorable thing to organise; browse the listings to pick a craft and date, and if you keep a boat on the river, you can list it too. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Antwerp Schelde or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Antwerp Schelde and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
